triggers.name: Linux: Configured max number of open filedescriptors is too low
Linux: node_exporter is not available
triggers.description: Failed to fetch system metrics from node_exporter in time.

triggers.name: Linux: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: Linux: System time is out of sync
triggers.description: The host's system time is different from Zabbix server time.

triggers.name: Linux: System name has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Linux by Prom/system.name[node_exporter],#1)<>last(/Linux by Prom/system.name[node_exporter],#2) and length(last(/Linux by Prom/system.name[node_exporter]))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Linux: Operating system description has changed
triggers.expression: last(/Linux by Prom/system.sw.os[node_exporter],#1)<>last(/Linux by Prom/system.sw.os[node_exporter],#2) and length(last(/Linux by Prom/system.sw.os[node_exporter]))>0
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Linux: {HOST.NAME} has been restarted
triggers.description: The device uptime is less than 10 minutes.

triggers.name: Linux: High memory utilization
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

items.name: Host name of Zabbix agent running

triggers.name: Windows: Number of free system page table entries is too low
triggers.expression: max(/Windows by Zabbix agent/perf_counter_en["\Memory\Free System Page Table Entries"],5m)<{$MEM.PAGE_TABLE_CRIT.MIN}
triggers.description: `Memory\Free System Page Table Entries` has been less than `{$MEM.PAGE_TABLE_CRIT.MIN}` for 5 minutes. If the number is less than 5,000, there may be a memory leak.

triggers.name: Windows: The Memory Pages/sec is too high
triggers.description: The Memory Pages/sec in the last 5 minutes exceeds `{$MEM.PAGE_SEC.CRIT.MAX}`. If the value is greater than 1,000, as a result of excessive paging, there may be a memory leak.

triggers.name: Windows: CPU interrupt time is too high
triggers.expression: min(/Windows by Zabbix agent/perf_counter_en["\Processor Information(_total)\% Interrupt Time"],5m)>{$CPU.INTERRUPT.CRIT.MAX}
triggers.description: The CPU Interrupt Time in the last 5 minutes exceeds `{$CPU.INTERRUPT.CRIT.MAX}`%.

triggers.name: Windows: CPU privileged time is too high
triggers.expression: min(/Windows by Zabbix agent/perf_counter_en["\Processor Information(_total)\% Privileged Time"],5m)>{$CPU.PRIV.CRIT.MAX}
triggers.description: The CPU privileged time in the last 5 minutes exceeds {$CPU.PRIV.CRIT.MAX}%.

triggers.name: Windows: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: Windows: System name has changed
triggers.expression: change(/Windows by Zabbix agent/system.hostname) and length(last(/Windows by Zabbix agent/system.hostname))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Windows: System time is out of sync
triggers.description: The host's system time is different from Zabbix server time.

triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.

items.key: wmi.getall[root\cimv2,"select Name,Description,NetConnectionID,Speed,AdapterTypeId,NetConnectionStatus,GUID from win32_networkadapter where PhysicalAdapter=True and NetConnectionStatus>0"]

triggers.name: Windows: Zabbix agent is not available
triggers.description: For passive agents only, host availability is used with `{$AGENT.TIMEOUT}` as a time threshold.

macros.description: Timeout after which agent is considered unavailable. Works only for agents reachable from Zabbix server/proxy (passive mode).

items.name: Host name of Zabbix agent running

triggers.name: Windows: Zabbix agent is not available
triggers.description: For active agents, `nodata()` with `agent.ping` is used with `{$AGENT.NODATA_TIMEOUT}` as a time threshold.

triggers.name: Windows: Number of free system page table entries is too low
triggers.expression: max(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/perf_counter_en["\Memory\Free System Page Table Entries"],5m)<{$MEM.PAGE_TABLE_CRIT.MIN}
triggers.description: `Memory\Free System Page Table Entries` has been less than `{$MEM.PAGE_TABLE_CRIT.MIN}` for 5 minutes. If the number is less than 5,000, there may be a memory leak.

triggers.name: Windows: The Memory Pages/sec is too high
triggers.description: The Memory Pages/sec in the last 5 minutes exceeds `{$MEM.PAGE_SEC.CRIT.MAX}`. If the value is greater than 1,000, as a result of excessive paging, there may be a memory leak.

triggers.name: Windows: CPU interrupt time is too high
triggers.expression: min(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/perf_counter_en["\Processor Information(_total)\% Interrupt Time"],5m)>{$CPU.INTERRUPT.CRIT.MAX}
triggers.description: The CPU Interrupt Time in the last 5 minutes exceeds `{$CPU.INTERRUPT.CRIT.MAX}`%.

triggers.name: Windows: CPU privileged time is too high
triggers.expression: min(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/perf_counter_en["\Processor Information(_total)\% Privileged Time"],5m)>{$CPU.PRIV.CRIT.MAX}
triggers.description: The CPU privileged time in the last 5 minutes exceeds {$CPU.PRIV.CRIT.MAX}%.

triggers.name: Windows: High CPU utilization
triggers.description: CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

triggers.name: Windows: System name has changed
triggers.expression: change(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/system.hostname) and length(last(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/system.hostname))>0
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Windows: Operating system description has changed
triggers.expression: change(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/system.sw.os) and length(last(/Windows by Zabbix agent active/system.sw.os))>0
triggers.description: The description of the operating system has changed. Possible reasons are that the system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.name: Windows: Host has been restarted
triggers.description: The device uptime is less than 10 minutes.

triggers.name: Windows: High memory utilization
triggers.description: The system is running out of free memory.
